title: rumex acetosella subsp. pyrenaicus
intro: Rumex acetosella subsp. pyrenaicus, also known as the sheep's sorrel or mountain sorrel, is a subspecies of the common sorrel plant, characterized by its distinctive red to yellow flowers and acidic taste. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its high levels of vitamin C and its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rumex acetosella subsp. pyrenaicus include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant. Rumex acetosella subsp. pyrenaicus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or supplement, and has been traditionally used in various herbal preparations, including salves, tinctures, and ointments.
